2: That I have I have yet to speak on God's behalf.
3: And will ascribe righteousness to my Maker.
4: He that is perfect in knowledge is is with thee.
5: He is He is mighty in strength and and wisdom.
6: But giveth right to the poor.
7: Yea, he doth establish them for ever, and they are exalted.
8: And And be holden in cords of affliction;
9: And their transgressions that they have exceeded.
10: And commandeth that they return from iniquity.
12: And they shall die without knowledge.
13: They cry not when he bindeth them.
14: And their life is is among the unclean.
15: And openeth their ears in oppression.
16: And that which should be set on thy table should be should be full of fatness.
17: Judgment and justice take hold on thee on thee .
18: Then a great ransom cannot deliver thee.
19: Nor all the forces of strength.
20: When people are cut off in their place.
21: For this hast thou chosen rather than affliction.
23: Or who can say, Thou hast wrought iniquity?
25: Man may behold it it afar off.
26: Neither can the number of his years be searched out.
27: They pour down rain according to the vapour thereof:
28: And And distil upon man abundantly.
29: Or Or the noise of his tabernacle?
30: And covereth the bottom of the sea.
32: And commandeth it not to shine not to shine by the cloud the cloud that cometh betwixt.
33: The cattle also concerning the vapour.
